knowledge.insight: KVD: 'Knowing the dominant baitfish is critical, especially in fall when bass target the most abundant species to fatten up. Forage varies even between lakes miles apart — bluegill, perch, alewives, smelt, shad, gobies. Regional differences affect coloration, seasonal patterns, migration, and habitat. Study biological reports, ask guides about dominant bait, note coloration and size during practice. Inspect regurgitated baitfish closely.'
